rationale: In the citing paper, Boyd, Chandy, and Cunha (2010) is used mainly as an example in a table of prior marketing event studies that may treat announcement-window CARs as event value, and once as a generic precedent for including firm size controls. The citing paper does not draw on the cited paper's customer power theory, CMO-specific mechanisms, or findings to develop its own hypotheses. Its core argument is built at a general event-study interpretation level across many studies, so this citation serves background/illustrative positioning rather than a distinct substantive dependency.
